title: Agent Coordination Batch Merge Authority v1
description: The declared merge authority for a batch, persisted additively in the batch manifest / launch metadata so the dashboard can render the batch and job Merge auth field. The pr-batch launch prompt already declares merge_authority; this contract is the canonical persisted form. merge_authority is a batch attribute rather than a standalone keyed record, so it carries no logical key of its own and inherits the identity of the batch manifest it rides on. This schema defines state only; capture, persistence, and UI behavior are separate concerns.

merge_authority.description: Canonical persisted merge authority. `auto` means the supervisor may merge when gates pass; `ask` means the supervisor surfaces one merge decision for the operator; `none` means the supervisor never merges. The pr-batch launch vocabulary maps to these canonical values before persistence: `auto_merge_when_gates_pass` -> `auto`, `ask` -> `ask`, `none` -> `none`. The verbose launch strings are not persisted, so there is a single canonical form per authority.

batch_manifest_projection.description: Embeddable additive fragment for a batch manifest / launch-metadata record. The manifest's existing properties remain valid beside this section. merge_authority is optional; when present it must be a canonical value. There is exactly one way to signal an undeclared authority — omit the property — so merge_authority is never null. An absent merge_authority degrades to an em-dash Merge auth field on the dashboard.
